SEARCH
Package com.nib.commons.io

Class AbstractFile

java.lang.Object
com.nib.commons.io.AbstractFile
Direct Known Subclasses:
AbstractVsamFile, DummyFile, SequentialFile, SortFile
public abstract class AbstractFile extends Object
Represents an abstract file with various operations for file handling and record management. This class serves as a base for specialized file implementations.

  • Field Details

    • exceptionsEnabled

      protected boolean exceptionsEnabled
      if true, file I/O error raise exceptions

    • openMode

      protected OpenMode openMode
      Represents the mode in which a file is opened. This can include modes such as read-only, write-only, append, input-output, and their optional variants. The openMode determines the operation(s) permitted on the file and how the file is accessed.

    • lockMode

      protected LockMode lockMode
      Specifies the locking mode used when accessing the file.

      The lock mode determines the type of access control applied to the file for concurrent operations. It can be one of the following: - Shared: Grants shared access to multiple readers. - SharedAutomatic: Automatically manages shared access. - Exclusive: Grants exclusive access to the file, preventing other operations.

    • handle

      protected FileDescriptor handle

    • ddName

      protected String ddName

    • context

      protected Context context

    • record

      protected Record record
      The file record area.

    • recordsRead

      protected long recordsRead
      The number of records read from the file since open.

    • recordsWritten

      protected long recordsWritten
      The number of records written to the file since open.

    • isOpen

      protected boolean isOpen

    • dataEncoding

      protected EncodingType dataEncoding
      Represents the encoding type used for data storage within the file. The variable determines whether the data is stored in Ascii or Ebcdic format based on the runtime configuration.

      This field is set during initialization, and its value is determined by the runtime configuration.

      It can be modified or retrieved as needed through the provided getter and setter methods.

    • recordEncoding

      protected EncodingType recordEncoding
      Specifies the encoding type of the file's records. The encoding is determined based on the runtime configuration, defaulting to EBCDIC if EBCDIC storage is enabled, otherwise defaults to ASCII.

      This property influences the behavior of record handling and interpretation during file operations where encoding type is relevant.

      The allowable encoding types are defined in the EncodingType enumeration, which includes: - EncodingType.Ascii - EncodingType.Ebcdic

      The runtime determination leverages a static configuration method RuntimeConfiguration.isEbcdicStorage().

    • fileStatus

      protected NZoned fileStatus

    • _status

      protected FileStatus _status

    • recordLimits

      protected int[] recordLimits

  • Constructor Details

    • AbstractFile

      public AbstractFile(FileDescriptor fileDescriptor)
      Constructs an AbstractFile using the provided file descriptor. This constructor attaches the file descriptor to the file instance and performs necessary initialization.
      Parameters:
      fileDescriptor - the file descriptor representing the file to be associated with this instance

    • AbstractFile

      public AbstractFile(Context context, FileDescriptor fileDescriptor)
      Constructs an AbstractFile object using the provided context and file descriptor. The file descriptor is associated with this instance, and necessary initialization is performed.
      Parameters:
      context - the program context
      fileDescriptor - the file descriptor representing the file to be associated with this instance

    • AbstractFile

      public AbstractFile(Context context, String ddName)
      Constructs an AbstractFile instance using the provided context and DD name. This constructor initializes necessary components associated with the given parameters.
      Parameters:
      context - the program context, typically used for maintaining state or configuration
      ddName - the DD (Data Definition) name representing the file or resource to be associated

  • Method Details

    • getFd

      public FileDescriptor getFd()
      Retrieves the file descriptor associated with this file.
      Returns:
      the file descriptor representing the file

    • isOpen

      public boolean isOpen()
      Checks if the file is currently open.
      Returns:
      true if the file is open, false otherwise

    • getDdName

      public String getDdName()
      Retrieves the Data Definition (DD) name associated with the file. If a handle is present, this method returns the DD name from the handle. Otherwise, it returns the locally stored DD name.
      Returns:
      the DD name as a String

    • exceptionsEnabled

      public AbstractFile exceptionsEnabled()
      Enables exception handling for the current file instance. This method sets the internal state to indicate that exceptions should be enabled and returns the current instance for method chaining.
      Returns:
      the current instance of AbstractFile with exceptions enabled

    • exceptionsDisabled

      public AbstractFile exceptionsDisabled()
      Disables exception handling for the current file instance. This method sets the internal state to indicate that exceptions should be disabled and returns the current instance for method chaining.
      Returns:
      the current instance of AbstractFile with exceptions enabled

    • recordIs

      public AbstractFile recordIs(AbstractNField record)
      Assigns a record to the file using the specified field. The record length is assumed equal to the field length.
      Parameters:
      record - the record field to assign
      Returns:
      the current instance of AbstractFile

    • recordIs

      public AbstractFile recordIs(AbstractNField record, NInteger recordLength)
      Assigns a record to the file using the specified field and record length.
      Parameters:
      record - the field to be processed and recorded
      recordLength - the length of the record to be processed
      Returns:
      the resulting AbstractFile after processing the record

    • recordIs

      public AbstractFile recordIs(AbstractNField record, EncodingType encoding)
      Assigns a record to the file using the specified field and encoding.
      Parameters:
      record - the record to be processed, represented as an AbstractNField object
      encoding - the encoding type to be applied, represented as an EncodingType object
      Returns:
      an AbstractFile object resulting from processing the provided record with the specified encoding

    • recordContains

      public AbstractFile recordContains(int min, int max)
      Specifies that record contains a number of bytes in the specified range.
      Parameters:
      min - minimum number of bytes in the record
      max - maximum number of bytes in the record
      Returns:

    • recordIs

      public AbstractFile recordIs(AbstractNField record, NInteger recordLength, EncodingType encoding)
      Assigns a record to the file using the specified field, length and encoding.
      Parameters:
      record - the file record assigned
      recordLength - the record length
      encoding - the encoding type
      Returns:
      itself

    • fileStatusIs

      public AbstractFile fileStatusIs(NGroup fileStatus)
      Assigns the file status field and returns teh current instance. Assigned field must be 2 bytes long.
      Parameters:
      fileStatus - the file status field
      Returns:

    • fileStatusIs

      public AbstractFile fileStatusIs(NChar fileStatus)
      Assigns the file status field and returns teh current instance. Assigned field must be 2 bytes long.
      Parameters:
      fileStatus - the file status field
      Returns:

    • fileStatusIs

      Assigns the file status field and returns teh current instance. Assigned field must be 2 bytes long.
      Parameters:
      fileStatus - the firled status field
      Returns:

    • encoding

      public AbstractFile encoding(EncodingType encoding)
      Sets the encoding type for the file and returns the current instance.
      Parameters:
      encoding - the desired encoding type to be applied
      Returns:
      the current instance of AbstractFile with the updated encoding type

    • errorDetected

      protected void errorDetected(Exception e)
      Handles an error scenario by updating the file status and potentially throwing an exception if exception handling is enabled.
      Parameters:
      e - the exception that was detected, used to determine the file status and to potentially re-throw if exceptions are enabled

    • getFileStatus

      public FileStatus getFileStatus()
      Retrieves the status of the file
      Returns:
      the current FileStatus of the file

    • setFileStatus

      protected void setFileStatus(FileStatus fs)
      Sets the file status for the current file and updates the associated fileStatus field if it is not null.
      Parameters:
      fs - the FileStatus object representing the new status to be set

    • errorDetected

      protected void errorDetected(FileIOException e)
      Handles the detection of a file error by updating the file status based on the exception and optionally rethrowing the exception if exceptions are enabled.
      Parameters:
      e - the FileIOException that contains details about the error, including its status

    • eofDetected

      protected void eofDetected()
      Handles the detection of the end of file (EOF) during file processing.

      This method is triggered when EOF is encountered during reading. If there is a next concatenated file available, it closes the current handle, switches to the next concatenated file, and attempts to reopen and read from it. If the reopening fails or if no further concatenated files are present, the method updates the file status to EOF. If exceptions are enabled, an EndOfFileException is thrown when EOF is reached.

      The method's workflow is expected to handle scenarios with concatenated files seamlessly by switching handles and continuing to process subsequent files. When no further files are available or an error occurs, it appropriately denotes the EOF status or raises an exception if configured.

    • close

      public FileIOResponse close() throws FileIOException
      Closes the file if it is currently open. If the file is already closed, an exception is thrown. Once closed, the file cannot be used until reopened.
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse object indicating the result of the close operation.
      Throws:
      FileIOException - if an error occurs while closing the file.
      FileAlreadyClosedException - if the file is already closed.

    • reopen

      protected FileIOResponse reopen()
      Reopens a file with the previously specified open mode and lock mode. This method is typically used to reopen a file after it has been closed or to reset certain file parameters as per the original configuration.
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse containing the result of the reopen operation, including the success status and any related information or errors.

    • readNext

      public FileIOResponse readNext() throws FileIOException, EndOfFileException
      Reads the next available record.
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse object that contains the result of the read operation.
      Throws:
      FileIOException - if an I/O error occurs during the file operation.
      EndOfFileException - if the end of the file is reached unexpectedly during the read operation.

    • open

      public FileIOResponse open(OpenMode openMode, LockMode lockMode) throws FileIOException
      Opens a file with the specified open and lock modes. Ensures the file handle and record length constraints are properly initialized. If the file is already open, this method will throw a FileAlreadyOpenException.
      Parameters:
      openMode - the mode in which the file should be opened, such as read, write, or append
      lockMode - the locking mode to apply while the file is open, such as shared or exclusive
      Returns:
      an instance of FileIOResponse representing the result of the open operation
      Throws:
      FileIOException - if an error occurs during the file operation
      FileAlreadyOpenException - if the file is already open
      InvalidRecordLengthException - if the record length constraints are invalid

    • readNext

      public abstract FileIOResponse readNext(boolean lock) throws FileIOException, EndOfFileException
      Reads the next available record from the file.
      Parameters:
      lock - a boolean value indicating whether the file should be locked during the read operation. If true, the file will be locked to prevent other processes from accessing it concurrently.
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse object containing the data read from the file.
      Throws:
      FileIOException - if an I/O error occurs during the operation.
      EndOfFileException - if the end of the file is reached and no more data is available to read.

    • dynalloc

      protected void dynalloc() throws FileIOException
      Dynamically allocates the file depending on DD This method determines the appropriate handle, open mode, and other resource configurations based on the input and runtime settings.
      Throws:
      FileIOException - If a file input/output operation fails.
      DdNotDefinedException - If the data descriptor name (ddName) is not defined in the current context.

    • checkPdsAsciiExtension

      public void checkPdsAsciiExtension()

    • currentRecordLength

      protected int currentRecordLength() throws IllegalRecordLengthException
      Calculates and returns the current record length. If the record has a variable length, it validates whether the length falls within the acceptable range defined by the handle. If the length is invalid, an IllegalRecordLengthException is thrown. For fixed-length records, the maximum length is returned.
      Returns:
      the current length of the record
      Throws:
      IllegalRecordLengthException - if the record length is variable and falls outside the defined minimum or maximum range

    • isVariableRecordLength

      protected boolean isVariableRecordLength()
      Determines if the record length is variable.
      Returns:
      true if the record format is not fixed, false otherwise

    • open

      public FileIOResponse open(OpenMode openMode) throws FileIOException
      Opens a file with the specified open mode and a default lock mode of Shared.
      Parameters:
      openMode - the mode in which the file is to be opened, such as Read or Write.
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse object representing the result of the file open operation.
      Throws:
      FileIOException - if an error occurs while attempting to open the file.

    • canRead

      protected boolean canRead()
      Determines if the current state allows reading operations.
      Returns:
      true if the object is open and the open mode is set to either input-only or input-output; false otherwise.

    • canWrite

      protected boolean canWrite()
      Determines if the current state and mode allow writing.
      Returns:
      true if the object is open and the open mode is not set to Input; false otherwise.

    • canRewrite

      protected boolean canRewrite()
      Determines if the current instance is eligible for rewrite operations.
      Returns:
      true if the instance is open and the open mode is set to InputOutput; false otherwise.

    • canDelete

      protected boolean canDelete()
      Determines whether the current object can be deleted based on its state.
      Returns:
      true if the object is open and its mode is InputOutput; false otherwise

    • readPrev

      public FileIOResponse readPrev() throws FileIOException, EndOfFileException
      Reads the previous record in the file. This method may throw exceptions if an error occurs during the operation or if there are no previous records to read.
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse object containing the data read from the previous record or chunk.
      Throws:
      FileIOException - if an I/O error occurs while reading.
      EndOfFileException - if there are no more records to read in the reverse direction.

    • readPrev

      public abstract FileIOResponse readPrev(boolean lock) throws FileIOException, EndOfFileException
      Reads the previous record in the file. This method provides functionality to navigate backward in the file while optionally locking it.
      Parameters:
      lock - a boolean value indicating whether to lock the file while the read operation is performed. If true, the file will be locked to prevent other modifications during reading.
      Returns:
      an instance of FileIOResponse containing the data read from the previous block or entry.
      Throws:
      FileIOException - if an I/O error occurs while attempting to read the file.
      EndOfFileException - if the beginning of the file is reached.

    • readNext

      public FileIOResponse readNext(AbstractNField into) throws FileIOException, EndOfFileException
      Reads the next record into the specified field.
      Parameters:
      into - an instance of AbstractNField where the next record's data will be read into. The data will be copied into this field subject to its length constraints.
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse indicating the result of the read operation, including success or failure.
      Throws:
      FileIOException - if an error occurs during the file input/output operation.
      EndOfFileException - if the end of the file is reached during the read operation.

    • write

      public FileIOResponse write(AbstractNField from) throws FileIOException
      Writes data from the specified AbstractNField to a file.
      Parameters:
      from - the AbstractNField containing the data to write
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse object indicating the result of the writing operation
      Throws:
      FileIOException - if an I/O error occurs during the write operation

    • from

      protected void from(AbstractNField from, int recordLength)
      Copies data from a given field into the current record, adjusting length and filling remaining space with blank characters if necessary.
      Parameters:
      from - The source field from which to copy data. It contains the storage, offset, and other relevant properties.
      recordLength - The length of the record to be copied, which determines how much data is transferred and how blanks are applied to fill extra space.

    • write

      public abstract FileIOResponse write() throws FileIOException
      Writes a record to the underlying file. This method performs the necessary operations to persist the content and ensures the data integrity during the write process.
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse object that provides details about the status of the write operation, such as success or failure information.
      Throws:
      FileIOException - if any I/O error occurs during the write process.

    • write

      public FileIOResponse write(AbstractNField from, int recordLength) throws FileIOException
      Writes the record from the specified field for teh specified length
      Parameters:
      from - record field
      recordLength - record length
      Returns:
      file IO response
      Throws:
      FileIOException

    • from

      protected void from(AbstractNField from)
      Copies the content from the specified AbstractNField source to the current instance.
      Parameters:
      from - the source AbstractNField to copy data from

    • printLine

      public FileIOResponse printLine(String format, Object... args)
      Formats the input string using the specified format and arguments, converts it to an appropriate character encoding based on the storage configuration, and writes the formatted line to file.
      Parameters:
      format - the format string used to create the formatted output
      args - the arguments referenced by the format specifiers in the format string
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse indicating the result of the write operation

    • getLastReadPosition

      public abstract Object getLastReadPosition()
      Retrieves the last read position in a given context. The specific details of the returned object depend on the concrete implementation of this method.
      Returns:
      an Object representing the last read position

    • checkRecordLength

      protected void checkRecordLength(int len) throws InvalidRecordLengthException
      Checks if the length of a record meets the specified constraints. If the record length is outside the allowable range, an exception is thrown.
      Parameters:
      len - the length of the record to check
      Throws:
      InvalidRecordLengthException - if the record length is invalid
